title: Core SOPs (21–40)
normalized_content:
SOP 21
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0113-R0
Title: Internal Audit and Self-Inspection Program
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0114-R0 (External Audit Management)
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0 (CAPA)
Related Forms:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1008-R0 (Audit Finding Record)
Related Products/Equipment: All
1. Purpose
To define the internal audit program for assessing GMP compliance and QMS effectiveness.
2. Scope
All GxP areas at PASH, VISK, and CORP functions.
3. Responsibilities
● QA: Annual audit plan, auditor qualification, report issuance.
● Auditees: Cooperation, CAPA implementation.
● CQO: Oversight and management review input.
4. Procedure
4.1 Audit Planning

1. Annual plan based on risk, changes, and prior findings.
2. Scope defined per audit (area, processes, period).
4.2 Execution
1. Opening meeting.
2. Document review, interviews, observations.
3. Daily debrief with auditee.
4.3 Reporting
1. Audit report issued within 10 working days.
2. Findings classified (Critical/Major/Minor/Observation).
4.4 CAPA and Follow-Up
1. Auditee proposes CAPA with due dates.
2. QA tracks closure and effectiveness.
5. Related Records
● Audit Plan (CORP-QA-MST-1113-R0)
● Audit Reports and Finding Registers
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 22
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0114-R0
Title: External Audit and Regulatory Inspection Management
Owner: Dr. Suresh Reddy

Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0113-R0
Related Products/Equipment: All
1. Purpose
To define preparation, conduct, and follow-up for external audits and regulatory inspections.
2. Scope
All regulatory, customer, and partner audits.
3. Responsibilities
● CQO: Overall accountability.
● QA: Coordination, response management.
● All Functions: Provide accurate information and records.
4. Procedure
4.1 Preparation
1. Confirm scope, agenda, and attendees.
2. Readiness check (documents, training, mock inspection if needed).
4.2 Conduct
1. Designated spokespersons per area.
2. Real-time logging of questions and commitments.
4.3 Follow-Up

1. Formal response to findings within agreed timelines.
2. CAPA initiation and tracking.
5. Related Records
● Inspection Reports
● Response Letters
● CAPA Files
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release CEO
SOP 23
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0117-R0
Title: Quality Risk Management
Owner: Dr. Suresh Reddy
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0 (CAPA)
Related Forms:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1007-R0 (Risk Assessment Form)
Related Products/Equipment: All

1. Purpose
To define the systematic application of quality risk management (QRM) across Aetheris.
2. Scope
All GxP processes, changes, deviations, and new projects.
3. Responsibilities
● Process Owners: Initiate risk assessments.
● QA: Facilitate QRM, maintain tools and templates.
● CQO: Oversight and escalation.
4. Procedure
4.1 Risk Assessment Initiation
1. Define scope, team, and risk question.
2. Use CORP-QA-FRM-1007 or FMEA templates.
4.2 Risk Analysis and Evaluation
1. Identify hazards, estimate risk (severity, occurrence, detection).
2. Compare against risk criteria.
4.3 Risk Control and Review
1. Define controls to reduce risk to acceptable level.
2. Periodic review of QRM outcomes.
5. Related Records
● Risk Assessment Reports
● FMEA Documents
● Linked CAPAs
6. Revision History

Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 24
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0118-R0
Title: Data Integrity Governance and Escalation
Owner: Dr. Suresh Reddy
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0
Related Products/Equipment: All GxP systems
1. Purpose
To establish governance for data integrity (DI) across Aetheris.
2. Scope
All GxP data, paper and electronic.
3. Responsibilities
● CQO: DI governance and culture.
● IT/System Owners: Technical controls.
● QA: Oversight, audit, and escalation.
4. Procedure
4.1 DI Principles

1. ALCOA+ embedded in all processes.
2. No tolerance for intentional data manipulation.
4.2 Controls
1. Access control, audit trails, backup.
2. Training on DI expectations.
4.3 Escalation
1. DI concerns escalated to CQO immediately.
2. Investigation per CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0.
5. Related Records
● DI Training Records
● DI Incident Logs
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release CEO
SOP 25
Document ID: PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0401-R0
Title: Receipt, Sampling, and Status Control of Incoming Materials
Owner: S. Naidu
Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0402-R0 (Storage)
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0301-R0 (Supplier Qualification)

Related Products: All FPPs and APIs (raw and packaging materials)
Related Equipment: Warehouse systems
1. Purpose
To define controls for receipt and status management of incoming materials.
2. Scope
All raw materials, packaging components, and APIs received at PASH.
3. Responsibilities
● Warehouse: Physical receipt and quarantine.
● QC: Sampling and testing.
● QA: Release/rejection decisions.
4. Procedure
4.1 Receipt
1. Verify delivery against purchase order.
2. Inspect for damage and labeling.
4.2 Quarantine and Sampling
1. Assign quarantine status in system.
2. QC samples drawn per sampling plan.
4.3 Status Update
1. QA updates status to “Released” or “Rejected.”
2. Warehouse moves materials accordingly.
5. Related Records
● Goods Receipt Notes

● COAs and Test Reports
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Rajesh Kulkarni
SOP 26
Document ID: PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0405-R0
Title: Printed Component Reconciliation and Issuance Control
Owner: S. Naidu
Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0401-R0
Related Products: FPP-1001/1002/1003/1004
Related Suppliers: SUP-002, SUP-008
1. Purpose
To prevent mix-ups and ensure accurate reconciliation of printed components.
2. Scope
All printed packaging materials (cartons, labels, leaflets).
3. Procedure
1. Segregated storage by product/batch.

2. Issuance against batch order only.
3. Reconciliation of issued, used, returned, and destroyed quantities.
4. Related Records
● Reconciliation Sheets
● Destruction Certificates
5. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Rajesh Kulkarni

SOP 32
Document ID: PASH-API-SOP-API-0306-R0
Title: Cleaning and Campaign Management in API Block
Owner: K. Ramesh
Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0209-R0 (Cleaning Validation)
Related Products: API-101/102/103
Related Equipment: EQ-API-01-001/002/003
1. Purpose
To define cleaning and campaign controls to prevent cross-contamination.
2. Scope
All API manufacturing equipment.
3. Procedure

1. Define campaign length per product.
2. Cleaning between campaigns or products as per validated procedures.
3. Verification via visual and analytical checks where required.
4. Related Records

● Campaign Plans
5. Revision History

SOP 36
Document ID: PASH-QC-SOP-QC-0505-R0
Title: OOS and OOT Management in QC Laboratories
Owner: Dr. Karthik Rao
Site: PASH
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● PASH-QC-SOP-QC-0502-R0
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
Related Products: All FPPs and APIs
1. Purpose
To define handling of out-of-specification (OOS) and out-of-trend (OOT) results.
2. Scope
All QC testing.
3. Procedure
1. Immediate notification to QA.
2. Phase 1 (lab) and Phase 2 (full) investigation.
3. CAPA initiation if root cause identified.
4. Related Records
● OOS/OOT Investigation Reports
● Lab Notebooks
5. Revision History

Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy

SOP 40
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0201-R0
Title: Validation Master Planning
Owner: Vikram Joshi
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0202-R0 (CSV)
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0208-R0 (Process Validation)
Related Products/Equipment: All

1. Purpose
To define the site-level validation master plan (VMP).
2. Scope
All validation activities (process, cleaning, equipment, CSV).
3. Procedure
1. Define validation policy and principles.
2. List systems/equipment/processes requiring validation.
3. Prioritize based on risk and regulatory impact.
4. Related Records

● Validation Summaries
